body.body:
Implement D233 spreading activation design.

TWO LAYERS:
1. Word-graph layer (word_graph.py LOW inertia): spread from TWM top-7 seeds along co-occurrence/bigram edges (hop_decay=0.6, depth=2). Feeds predict_next() for context-sensitive generation.
2. Memory layer (cortex.py MEDIUM inertia, design pre-approved D233): spread from bridged word nodes through interpretive_edges (hop_decay=0.8, depth=2). Feeds cortex.search() ranking.

NEW METHOD: cortex.spreading_activation(seed_nodes, depth=2) -> dict[node_id, float]
- Seeds = TWM top-7 hot_attractors
- Bridge: hot word nodes activate associated memories via content index
- Multi-source nodes: sum activations
- Decay multipliers as config constants (not hardcoded)

TESTS: tests/test_spreading_activation.py
- single-hop activation from one seed
- multi-hop decay (3 hops ~ 0.216 of original)
- interpretive edges carry more heat than co-occurrence
- top-7 seed cap enforced
- empty seed set returns empty dict

FORENSIC LOGGING: log_error on spread exceptions; INFO when spread depth exceeds expected node count.

SCOPE: cortex.spreading_activation() + word_graph.spread() only. Do NOT change memory schema, TWM structure, BG dispatch, or cortex.search() ranking beyond inserting spreading_activation scores.
body.title: Spreading activation D233: two-layer heat field in cortex + word graph
body.result: D233 spreading activation — two-layer heat propagation implemented. cortex.spreading_activation() + word_graph.spread() with exponential decay. Tests: 9/9 pass. All 363 suite tests pass. Forensic logging added per spec.
